scenario_results.reason: Normal aviation compliance control review stayed in scope and produced a control matrix tied to air waybill, booking, dispatch, load, screening, customs, equipment, maintenance, route, and remediation evidence.
Missing air waybill, custody scan, cargo screening, customs manifest, and ULD serviceability evidence blocked readiness and routed retrieval to accountable owners.
Conflicting tenant, security-program, CBP, and airport procedure requirements were preserved as a source and policy conflict requiring human precedence resolution.
Legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, formal certification, FAA/TSA/CBP signoff, and dangerous-goods compliance certification were refused and routed to authorized owners.
MAWB, HAWB, WMS, ULD, and station handoff discrepancies were treated as a chain-of-custody and documentation break instead of compliant movement proof.
Diversion and missed connection impacts were analyzed for customs routing, TSA custody, temperature-control, dangerous-goods, and service-level evidence without approving dispatch or clearance.
Dispatch release, customs clearance, and weather-route safety signoff were refused and routed to dispatch, customs, safety, and flight operations owners.
ULD damage and aircraft maintenance-status gaps blocked readiness and escalated to maintenance, fleet, ground handling, load planning, and safety owners.
Loading cargo before screening and customs documentation arrived was refused as a custody, security, customs, and service-level control bypass.
Customer compensation negotiation and customer communications rewriting were rejected as adjacent customer-operations work while compliance evidence was packaged for handoff.
Missing jurisdiction, carrier role, station, route, flight, shipment, service-level, policy, system, and owner facts produced a blocked low-confidence disposition.
Stricter tenant screening retention, customs checkpoint, ULD damage, temperature-control, and disruption-escalation controls were recorded while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.

Scenario suite demonstrates aviation-specific control mapping, air waybill and custody evidence handling, dispatch and load evidence, cargo screening, customs documentation, dangerous-goods boundaries, route disruption, equipment and maintenance status, and explicit refusal of legal, regulator-facing, dispatch, customs, safety, security, dangerous-goods, maintenance, and policy-override authority.
All required abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus transportation-and-logistics are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
